source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Gas Leak at WD 73AT

actions_taken: All 26 personnel mustered upwind in quarters building
Pipeline pressure bled down from 1080 psi to 0 psi
Clean Gulf Associates placed on standby
Portable gas detectors deployed for continuous monitoring
Diesel generator started at D platform
event.summary: On 17 February 2023 at approximately 0315 hours, a gas leak was discovered on a 12-inch gas pipeline riser (PSN 759), approximately 5-7 feet above the water line on the WD 73AT platform. The platform's ESD was activated, shutting down the facility and sounding the alarm. All 26 personnel on board were accounted for and sheltered in place in the quarters building upwind from the leak. The pipeline was bled down over several hours. A light sheen was observed during overflight, later determined to originate from the slop oil tank rather than the pipeline. Investigation revealed inadequate preventative maintenance, failed repairs documented in a 2015 survey, and insufficient pipeline inspection methods.
recommendations: Implement adequate preventative maintenance programs for pipeline risers
Ensure documented completion of repairs identified in inspection surveys
Utilize intelligent Pipeline Inspection Gauge (PIG) with magnetic flux leakage, ultrasound, and caliper technology
Ensure protective coating is applied to pipeline risers at hanger locations
Conduct Level 1 surveys with sufficient examination methods beyond drone inspection
key_phrases: failed to provide documents showing repairs were made
drone inspection did not provide sufficient examination

hazards.name: Corrosion and metal loss in pipeline

threats.name: Inadequate preventative maintenance of pipeline riser
Failure to complete documented repairs at waterline
Lack of protective coating at riser hanger

consequences.name: Facility shutdown and loss of production

controls.name: Level 1 Pipeline Survey
controls.barrier_role: Detection of pipeline damage and degradation
controls.lod_basis: Preventive inspection to identify issues before failure

supporting_text: The most recent Level 1 survey was completed on 15 February 2023 and showed no issues or damage to the PSN 759 riser or riser clamps
During the survey, a drone was used to inspect PSN 759 and did not provide sufficient examination of the pipeline

controls.name: Level 2 Pipeline Survey with Cathodic Potential and Riser Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Detailed inspection of pipeline coating and corrosion protection
controls.lod_basis: Comprehensive inspection to identify corrosion and coating damage

supporting_text: The Level 2 survey completed on 11 December 2015 inspected the Cathodic Potential (CP) and Riser/J-Tube
The diver was unable to repair damage at the waterline due to rough seas
Cox could not provide documentation stating the repairs at the waterline were ever made

controls.name: Pipeline Protective Coating (Concrete and Synthoglass)
controls.barrier_role: Protection against corrosion and environmental degradation
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to prevent corrosion of pipeline steel

supporting_text: Pipeline riser was not coated with protective lining at the riser hanger
The riser is concrete coated, and the damage noted was to the concrete coating
The riser was cleaned and wrapped with Synthoglass at -15 feet, -107 feet, and 109 feet

controls.name: Intelligent Pipeline Inspection Gauge (PIG) with MFL and Ultrasound
controls.barrier_role: Internal pipeline inspection to detect metal loss, corrosion, and cracks
controls.lod_basis: Advanced technology to identify internal pipeline degradation before failure

supporting_text: Another contributing factor to the incident discovered in BSEE's investigation was the lack of use of an intelligent Pipeline Inspection Gauge (PIG) with technology capabilities of magnetic flux leakage (MFL), ultrasound, and/or calipers
Using this type of PIG could have identified metal loss, corrosion, cracks, or any other contributing factors within the pipeline

controls.name: Emergency Shutdown System (ESD)
controls.barrier_role: Rapid facility shutdown to isolate hazard and protect personnel
controls.lod_basis: Automatic system to stop operations and alert personnel upon detection of emergency

supporting_text: The ESD was pulled, shutting down the entire facility including electrical power, and sounding the alarm for all personnel to report to their muster station

controls.name: Personnel Muster and Sheltering in Place
controls.barrier_role: Protection of personnel from gas exposure
controls.lod_basis: Procedural response to move personnel to safe location upwind of hazard

supporting_text: All 26 POB were accounted for and remained sheltered in place in the quarters building located upwind from the leak
Due to weather conditions and the winds blowing the leaked gas away from the living quarters, WD 73Q, it was determined that sheltering inside WD 73Q was the safest option at the time

controls.name: Portable Gas Detectors and Continuous Monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detection and monitoring of gas concentration to ensure personnel safety
controls.lod_basis: Real-time detection of hazardous gas levels during incident response

supporting_text: Two production operators remained on station with portable gas detectors for continuous monitoring, rotating personnel every 2 hours

controls.name: Pipeline Pressure Bleed Down Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Reduction of pressure in pipeline to stop gas release
controls.lod_basis: Procedural response to depressurize pipeline and terminate gas release

supporting_text: At approximately 0330 to 0400 hours, a temporary means of communication was established and a call was made to Grand Isle (GI) 18A to bleed down the pipeline which was at around 1080 psi
On 18 February 2023 at 0015 hours, GI 18A confirmed the pipeline pressure for PSN 759 was at 0 psi

controls.name: Emergency Response Plan and External Notifications
controls.barrier_role: Coordination of emergency response and regulatory notification
controls.lod_basis: Procedural activation of emergency response and notification of authorities

supporting_text: Cox activated their emergency response plan, notifying their partnership with Forefront Emergency Management (FEM)
At 0435 hours, the National Response Center (NRC) was notified, and a report was issued (NRC#1360214)
At 0445 hours, FEM began notifications to multiple state and federal agencies

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
